title: Support nonconsecutive repetition [=N] in sequence expressions
body:
## Summary

This patch adds support for the nonconsecutive repetition operator `[=N]`
in SVA concurrent assertions (IEEE 1800-2023 Section 16.9.2).

Also renames `AstConsRep` to `AstSConsRep` and consolidates repetition
error/unsupported tests into shared files for consistency.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t (input clk);
  logic a, b;
  assert property (@(posedge clk) a[=2] |-> b)
    else $error("FAIL");
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: Unsupported: [= boolean abbrev expression
```

## Changes

- Add `AstSNonConsRep` node in `V3AstNodeExpr.h` (parallel to `AstSGotoRep`).
- Move `[=N]` from `boolean_abbrev` to the `sexpr` rule in `verilog.y`; range
  form `[=M:N]` remains unsupported.
- Add type-check visitor in `V3Width.cpp`.
- Refactor `createGotoRepPExpr` to `createRepPExpr(isNonConsec)` in
  `V3AssertPre.cpp`. When `isNonConsec`, adds trailing `##1` + `!b[*0:$]`
  window loop per IEEE equivalence `b[=N] = b[->N] ##1 !b[*0:$]`.
- Rename `validateGotoRepCount` to `validateRepCount` (shared by goto and
  nonconsec). Add negative count check.
- Add `SNonConsecRep` to `throughout` guard in `V3AssertProp.cpp`.
- Rename `AstConsRep` to `AstSConsRep` for consistent `AstS*` naming across
  all three repetition operators.

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_assert_nonconsec_rep.{v,py}` -- CRC functional
  test with `|->`, `|=>`, and standalone forms.
- Added `test_regress/t/t_assert_rep_bad_count.{v,py,out}` -- merged error
  paths for goto, nonconsec, and consec (non-constant, zero, negative counts).
- Added `test_regress/t/t_assert_rep_unsup.{v,py,out}` -- merged unsupported
  forms for consec and nonconsec (throughout, trailing range, non-`##1` delay).
- Renamed `t_assert_prop_consec_rep` to `t_assert_consec_rep` for consistent
  naming with `t_assert_goto_rep` / `t_assert_nonconsec_rep`.
- Removed `t_assert_goto_rep_bad_count`, `t_assert_prop_consec_rep_bad`,
  `t_assert_prop_consec_rep_unsup` (merged into shared files above).
- Updated `t_sequence_sexpr_unsup` golden -- `[=N]` no longer unsupported;
  added `[=M:$]` unbounded range case.

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
